description: An icon-only or custom control was visually obvious but had no accessible name or keyboard behavior.
body: Failure mode: mouse users can infer and click an icon, but screen reader users and role-based tests cannot identify it, or keyboard users cannot activate it. Diagnostic trigger: controls are implemented with bare SVGs, divs, spans, or buttons whose only content is decorative. Do-not-use signal: do not accept visual affordance as sufficient semantics. Recovery: prefer a native button or link, add visible text or a precise accessible name, preserve focus visibility, and test keyboard activation. Risk boundary: generic labels such as 'button' or duplicated aria labels are still failures when several controls share the same name.
